DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUL-31-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ERICA WALTERS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES, REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER ANSWERS TO: SUPPLEMENTAL INTERROGATORIES, SUPPLEMENTAL REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SET 2 AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE PRO TEM: ROBERT KANE; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Defendant Erica Walters? Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-31-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Defendant Erica Walters' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Answers to: Supplemental Interrogatories, Supplemental Request for Production of Documents, and Special Interrogatories set 2 and for Sanctions is granted. Responses were belated filed and not verified consequently all objections were waived. (CCP 2030.090(a), 2031.300(a).) As no request for wavier was made pursuant to CCP 2030.290(a) or 2031.300, all objections are waived . Plaintiff to provide verified responses, without objection, to Supplemental Interrogatories, Supplemental Requests for Production and Special Interrogatories set number two (numbered 16-19) on or before August 17, 2020. Since no opposition was filed no sanctions will be awarded.

The prevailing party is required to submit to the judge pro tem a proposed order which repeats the court's ruling.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Defendant Erica Walters' Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters In Request For Admissions, Set Two, Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-31-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Defendant Erica Walters' Motion To Deem Matters In Request For Admissions, Set Two, Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's Motion to Deem Matters in Request of Admissions Set #2 Deemed Admitted and for Sanctions is granted. Although no opposition was filed, Defendant admits that Plaintiff, who had recently been required to represent himself, had filed verified responses to the discovery at issue with belated objections. As a result, the objections are deemed waived for failing to have been timely asserted pursuant to CCP 2033.280(a). Although Plaintiff, having filed a verified response, could make a request for relief from waiver under CCP 2033.300, no request was made. Therefore, the Requests for Admission set forth in number 2 (numbered 16-28) are deemed admitted. Although no opposition was filed, sanctions are mandatory pursuant to CCP 2033.280(c) and will be awarded to Defendant in the amount of $560.

The prevailing party is required to submit to the judge pro tem a proposed order which repeats the court's ruling.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
